entries.title: Dwarven Honor Memory And Craft Shape Conduct

context.label: Dwarven Honor Memory And Craft Shape Conduct

content.fact: Dwarven conduct is shaped by kinship, craft, old wrongs, treasured works, and durable loyalty once earned.
content.injection: Use Dwarven pride and craft-memory as social forces, not simple stubbornness.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

content.fact: Elves possess long memory and beauty, but their power in Middle-earth is fading and many are preparing to depart.
content.injection: Elven scenes should carry age, memory, restraint, and loss instead of endless intervention.

content.fact: Hobbits are sheltered and provincial, but many are observant, resilient, practical, and morally courageous.
content.injection: Write Hobbits as local, social, food-conscious, and cautious without making them stupid or helpless.

content.fact: Hope is often an act of endurance and duty despite incomplete knowledge, not cheerful certainty.
content.injection: Hopeful choices should feel disciplined and costly; characters may act rightly without expecting survival.

entries.title: Aragorn Carries A Hidden Royal Claim

context.label: Aragorn's hidden claim before kingship is established

content.fact: Aragorn is heir of Isildur and carries a royal claim, but that claim is revealed and accepted gradually rather than treated as common public fact.
content.injection: Keep his royal claim weighty and context-dependent. He may act with hidden authority before others fully know or accept who he is.
constraints: The claim has symbolic, political, and moral weight; it is not just a secret name.
Acceptance of the claim depends on audience, place, timing, and deeds.
antiLore: Do not make every Gondorian, Rohirrim, or traveler automatically accept Aragorn as king early in the story.
Do not separate Aragorn's claim from his restraint, burden, and need to prove legitimacy.

entries.title: Frodo Is The Ring-bearer After Bilbo

context.label: Frodo carries the Ring during the War of the Ring

content.fact: After Bilbo leaves the Shire, Frodo becomes the keeper of Bilbo's ring and later the Ring-bearer responsible for carrying the One Ring.
content.injection: During the War of the Ring, treat Frodo as the Ring-bearer after Bilbo. His role is a private burden, not a public title most people understand.
constraints: Frodo may initially understand the Ring incompletely before Gandalf confirms its nature.
His responsibility should feel isolating even when companions support him.
antiLore: Do not leave Bilbo as the active Ring-bearer during the main War of the Ring journey.
Do not portray Frodo's role as public knowledge across Middle-earth.

entries.title: Gandalf Operates As Guide Not Ruler

context.label: Gandalf's role during the War of the Ring

content.fact: Gandalf acts as a guide, counselor, and catalyst for resistance to Sauron rather than as a ruler who dominates the Free Peoples.
content.injection: Portray him as wise, urgent, and forceful when needed, but not as someone who simply commands Middle-earth through raw power or political rule.
constraints: Gandalf can lead in crisis, but his deeper role is to awaken courage, judgment, and resistance in others.
He refuses corrupting shortcuts such as wielding the One Ring.
antiLore: Do not portray Gandalf as an all-powerful wizard who solves conflicts by direct magical domination.
Do not make him politically rule Gondor, Rohan, the Shire, or the Fellowship.

entries.title: Gollum Is Bound To The Ring By Possession

context.label: Gollum's pursuit of the Ring during the War of the Ring

content.fact: Gollum is psychologically and spiritually bound to the One Ring after long possession, mixing hatred, need, cunning, fear, and dependence.
content.injection: Portray his relationship to the Ring as obsessive and damaged. He may be pitiable, treacherous, cunning, servile, or desperate, but the Ring remains his central fixation.
constraints: Gollum can show divided impulses through Smeagol/Gollum tension.
His fixation should affect trust, speech, choices, and risk-taking.
antiLore: Do not make Gollum simply a loyal guide without danger.
Do not make him casually indifferent to the Ring or to Frodo carrying it.

context.label: Sam accompanies Frodo during the War of the Ring

content.fact: Samwise Gamgee is Frodo's loyal companion, grounded by practical courage, emotional devotion, and a strong protective instinct.
content.injection: In Frodo and Sam scenes, treat Sam's loyalty as active and practical: he protects, worries, serves, questions danger, and keeps Frodo moving when the burden becomes heavy.
constraints: Sam's loyalty should not make him passive; he can challenge, doubt, or resist others when Frodo is at risk.
His perspective is grounded, plain-spoken, and morally steady.
antiLore: Do not reduce Sam to comic relief or simple obedience.
Do not portray Sam as indifferent to the Ring's effect on Frodo.

content.injection: Post-war scenes should preserve cost; celebration can coexist with permanent wounds and departures.
